conservative.description: Uses maximum thresholds for best separation

implementation_notes.inverted: Use if your empty state truly has higher variance than occupied
implementation_notes.percentile: Safe choice - uses statistical percentile boundaries
implementation_notes.ratio: Best for variable environments - uses relative changes
implementation_notes.energy: Simple and direct - works well with stable data
implementation_notes.conservative: Most cautious - maximizes separability at cost of precision

how_to_use.step_2: Apply thresholds in pattern_detector.py or app.py
how_to_use.step_3: Run live calibration if needed (app.py web interface)
how_to_use.step_4: Monitor accuracy and adjust if necessary
